//: Worked example: connect McpLense to a Microsoft Agent365 MCP endpoint via Entra ID.
//1: This file is an *auth profile* config (Phase A): it describes HOW to authenticate, not
//2: WHICH server to connect to. The same profile services every Agent365 MCP under a tenant
//5: Drop this file into one of:
//8: or pass it explicitly with: mcplense inspect <url> --profiles ./agent365.json
//10: On Windows without XDG_CONFIG_HOME, McpLense falls back to %APPDATA%\McpLense.
//11: On Linux/macOS without XDG_CONFIG_HOME, McpLense falls back to ~/.config/McpLense.
//16: First-time login pops a browser; subsequent commands reuse a token cached in the OS
//17: credential store (DPAPI on Windows, Keychain on macOS, libsecret on Linux).
//18: Requires the env vars VSCODE_CLIENT_ID / CORP_TENANT_ID / VSCODE_AUDIENCE to be set.
//21: Delegates to the Azure CLI: McpLense calls 'az account get-access-token --resource
//22: <scope>' for every request. No interactive browser pop, no MSAL cache. Requires:
//23: 1. Azure CLI installed and on PATH.
//25: 3. The signed-in account has access to the target Entra app.
//26: Ideal for headless / CI / SSH-only environments where no browser is available.
//27: Requires the env vars CORP_TENANT_ID / VSCODE_AUDIENCE to be set; the CLI uses its
//28: own pre-registered client id so VSCODE_CLIENT_ID is NOT needed for this profile.
//30: Pick exactly one profile per invocation:
//33: When only one of the two profiles is loaded (e.g. you delete the other entry),
//34: McpLense picks it automatically and --profile is optional.

targetPatterns.//: Wildcard match against any URL whose host contains 'bluebird'. The ** glob
targetPatterns.//1: spans labels + dots so this catches mcp.bluebird-ai.net AND any other bluebird
targetPatterns.//2: subdomain. scope=All means the three x-mcp-ec-* headers ride along with both the
targetPatterns.//3: MCP session and the same-origin probes (transport probe, CORS preflight,
targetPatterns.//4: authenticated-headers re-probe, DCR endpoint when same-origin).
